meta.note: Captured from a real seeded run (auth enabled). Do not hand-edit; regenerate with CAPTURE_WRITE=1.

steps.instructions: Ingest the day's device complaints and propose reportability per complaint: escalate deaths, serious injuries, and malfunctions likely to recur, with the applicable MDR clock and a rationale.
Draft the MDR report skeletons for the officer-decided reportable complaints and notify the regulatory channel.

complaints.description: Reading discrepancy traced to expired test strips supplied by the patient
Discoloration on tubing exterior with no functional impact found on inspection
Device not charged before use and stopped mid-session
Pump over-delivered insulin overnight and patient was hospitalized with severe hypoglycemia
Outer pouch seal wrinkled but sterile barrier intact on inspection
Strap fitted upside down per submitted photos with no injury reported
Adhesive left residue on skin and resolved with a cleaning wipe
Low-pressure alarm failed to sound during a circuit disconnect with no patient harm this time
Catheter kinked during insertion by an untrained user and was replaced without incident
Single inaccurate reading that could not be reproduced and device within spec on retest

drafts.draft: MDR draft for C-3004 (InsuFlow MX insulin pump): serious_injury. Escalation rationale: serious injury (patient harm: hospitalized) — reportable event; the 30-calendar-day MDR clock under 21 CFR 803.50 runs from awareness (2026-06-02). MDR draft for C-3008 (VentAssist 300 ventilator): malfunction. Escalation rationale: malfunction with high recurrence risk — would be likely to cause or contribute to a death or serious injury if it recurred; malfunction reportable under 21 CFR 803.50 from awareness (2026-06-04). Clock: 30-day malfunction MDR (21 CFR 803.50). Reportability decided by the regulatory officer at the reportability_decision gate.
